name: No-Knead European Breads (Kolaches and Dinner
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine yeast, warm milk, warm water, sugar, butter or margarine, salt, and eggs. Mix with an electric mixer on low speed for 3 minutes, scraping the bowl constantly.
2. Stir in enough of the remaining flour with a wooden spoon to make a soft dough that's not sticky. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
3. Punch down the dough. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface.
4. Divide dough into two halves. Cover and let rest for 10 minutes.
5. Shape as desired.
6. Bake according to directions.
PARKER HOUSE ROLLS:
7. Prepare dough as directed.
8. Lightly grease baking sheets.
9. Melt 2 tablespoons butter or margarine.
10. On a lightly floured surface, roll each half of dough about 1/4 inch thick.
11. Cut with a floured 2-1/2-inch round cutter.
12. Brush each dough round with some melted butter or margarine.
13. Make an off-center crease in each round. Fold each round along the crease so the larger half is on top.
14. Press folded edges firmly.
15. Place rolls, 2-3 inches apart on baking sheets.
16. Cover and let rise in a warm place until nearly doubled, about 30 minutes.
17. Bake in a 375°F oven for 12-15 minutes or until golden brown. (Makes 36 rolls.)
BUTTERHORN ROLLS:
18. Prepare dough as directed.
19. Lightly grease baking sheets.
20. Melt 2 tablespoons butter or margarine.
21. On a lightly floured surface, roll each half of dough into a 12-inch circle.
22. Brush with melted butter or margarine.
23. Cut each circle into 12 wedges.
24. Begin at the wide end of each wedge and roll toward the point.
25. Place, point side down, 2-3 inches apart on baking sheets.
26. Cover and let rise in a warm place until nearly doubled, about 30 minutes.
27. Bake in a 375°F oven for 12-15 minutes or until golden brown. (Makes 24 rolls.)
KOLACHES:
28. Prepare dough as directed.
29. Lightly grease baking sheet.
30. On a lightly floured surface, shape each half of dough into 12 balls, pulling the edges under to make a smooth top.
31. Place the balls 3 inches apart on baking sheets.
32. Shape each ball to about 2-1/2 inches in diameter.
33. Cover and let rise in a warm place until nearly doubled, about 35 minutes.
34. Using your thumb or two fingers, make an indentation in the center of each dough circle.
35. Spoon 2 teaspoons purchased poppy seed or fruitcake and pastry filling into each indentation.
36. Lightly brush 2 tablespoons melted butter or milk around edges of rolls.
37. Bake in a 375°F oven for 12-15 minutes or until rolls are golden brown. Remove to rack. Sift about 1 tablespoon powdered sugar over rolls. (Makes 24 rolls.)
